hero.title: Learn practical AI for real work.
hero.subtitle: Beginner-friendly lessons, templates, and AI workflow services using ChatGPT, Claude, and Google Sheets.

problem.title: Does this sound like you?
problem.subtitle: You don't have a technical problem. You have a starting-point problem. Once you know the right tools and the right order, everything clicks.
problem.hint: You don't need to become a developer.
problem.hintHighlight: You just need the right system.
problem.item0desc: You copy the same data, send the same emails, and follow the same steps every day. It works — but it takes hours you don't have.
problem.item1desc: Every week there's a new AI tool, a new trend, a new thing to learn. It's hard to know where to start or what actually matters for your work.
problem.item2title: Using AI like a search bar
problem.item2desc: You use ChatGPT or Claude, but only for basic questions. You know there's more it can do — you just haven't figured out how yet.
problem.item3desc: AI is changing how people work, fast. You want to stay confident, relevant, and ahead — without needing a tech degree to do it.

method.title: Two tools. One system.
method.subtitle: Most people try to use AI and automation separately. The real power is combining them into a single, connected workflow.
method.part1desc: Automation handles the repetitive work — the copy-paste, the data entry, the follow-up emails, the scheduling. Set it up once, and it runs by itself every time.
method.part1item0: Triggers, actions, and logic — no code
method.part1item3: Works while you focus on real work
method.part2desc: AI handles the thinking work — writing, summarising, analysing, planning. It drafts emails, summarises reports, and suggests next steps based on your instructions.
method.part2item1: AI agents that work across multiple steps
method.part2item3: Safe, honest, and useful — not just hype
method.quote: "Automation handles the repetitive work. AI helps with the thinking work.
method.quoteHighlight: Together, they change how you work."

curriculum.title: What you'll learn
curriculum.subtitle: Four structured parts that take you from "I don't know where to start" to building workflows you actually use every day.
curriculum.bottomNote: Every part ends with something you can use immediately in your work.
curriculum.bottomNoteHighlight: No fluff, no theory for its own sake.
curriculum.part0tagline: Understand how automation works — and build your first real workflow.
curriculum.part0item0: What automation is and when to use it
curriculum.part0item2: APIs and webhooks explained in plain English
curriculum.part0item3: Planning your first workflow step by step
curriculum.part1tagline: Use AI for real work tasks — emails, reports, research, and planning.
curriculum.part1item0: AI for emails, reports, notes, research, and planning
curriculum.part1item1: Prompting strategies for practical work tasks
curriculum.part1item3: When to use AI — and when not to
curriculum.part2tagline: Go beyond basic chatting — build AI that actually does work for you.
curriculum.part2item0: The difference between an assistant and an agent
curriculum.part3tagline: Build four complete real-world workflows from start to finish.

projects.title: Learn by building
projects.subtitle: This is not just educational theory. Every module ends with a real workflow you can use in your actual work — starting on day one.
projects.proj0desc: Connect your inbox to an AI that reads, categorises, and drafts replies to common messages. Spend minutes, not hours, in your email.
projects.proj1desc: Turn a simple idea list into a structured content calendar. AI suggests topics, angles, and post formats based on your audience.
projects.proj2desc: When someone fills out a form or books a call, the system logs the lead, sends a personalised follow-up, and reminds your team.
projects.proj3desc: Pull data from a spreadsheet, pass it to AI, and generate a clean written summary or weekly report — automatically, on schedule.
projects.proj4desc: A central view of all your active automations and AI tasks. Track what's running, what's completed, and what needs your attention.
projects.proj5desc: Upload a PDF, contract, or long document. AI extracts the key points, action items, and a plain-English summary in seconds.

whoItsFor.title: Who this training is for
whoItsFor.subtitle: This training was built for people who work hard and want to work smarter — not for engineers or data scientists.
whoItsFor.noRequirementsTitle: You do not need any of these to start
whoItsFor.noRequirementsSubtitle: If you use a computer for work, you have everything you need.
whoItsFor.aud0desc: Admin, marketing, operations, HR, project coordinators, remote workers, and knowledge workers who want practical AI skills without needing to code.
whoItsFor.aud1desc: YouTube creators, newsletter writers, and digital product sellers who want faster, smarter content workflows without spending all day on production.
whoItsFor.aud2desc: Owners who need simple, reliable systems for leads, follow-ups, bookings, customer replies, content, and reports — without hiring a developer.

resources.title: Free templates & tools
resources.subtitle: Start before the full training. Practical resources you can use right now to begin saving time and building better workflows.
resources.starterDesc: Prompts, templates, and a beginner checklist — everything you need to start this week, free.
resources.res0desc: A curated pack of beginner-friendly prompts, tool recommendations, and a step-by-step checklist to start using AI in your work this week.
resources.res1desc: Ready-to-use prompts for emails, reports, meeting summaries, content ideas, research, and more. Copy, paste, and customise.
resources.res2desc: Pre-built workflow blueprints for the most common automation setups. Import directly into Make or Zapier and customise for your needs.
resources.res3desc: Simple checklists to help you plan, test, and launch your first automations without skipping important steps.
resources.res4desc: Short, focused guides covering specific tools and topics: Getting started with Make, Understanding AI agents, Writing effective prompts, and more.
resources.res5desc: A plain-English breakdown of the top AI and automation tools — what each does, when to use it, and which ones are worth your time.

about.title: Practical AI education
about.desc1: Maddy the Techie teaches non-technical professionals how to use AI and automation in real work situations. The goal is to make modern tools simple, useful, and approachable.
about.desc2: You don't need to understand how AI works under the hood. You need to know how to use it confidently in your daily work — and that's exactly what this training delivers.
about.subtitle: AI & Automation Educator
about.tagline: "Making AI practical for everyone who doesn't code."
about.belief0: You don't need to code to work smarter with AI.
about.belief1: Every lesson should end with something you can actually use.
about.belief2: The best AI workflow is one you understand and can maintain yourself.
about.belief3: Practical beats theoretical, every single time.

cta.title: Ready to make AI
cta.subtitle: Start learning practical AI and automation skills you can use in your daily work — without coding or confusing tech jargon.

footer.tagline: Practical AI & automation for modern work
footer.motto: Learn AI. Automate Work. No Coding.
